# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name FROM products WHERE brand='3M' AND series LIKE '%Ventilated SecureFit 2CE-B35%' AND name LIKE '%Safety Eyewear%' AND json_extract(properties,'$.lens_color')='Clear' AND json_extract(properties,'$.size')='M' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, line, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type') AS fastener_type, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS diameter_mm, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter FROM products WHERE brand='Heco' AND line LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated HECO 3DW-64B Nut Bolt and Washer%' AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt' AND (json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm')='8' OR json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm')=8 OR json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm' OR json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='8mm') LIMIT 10;

response.stderr: SQL logic error: no such column: line (1)

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, brand, name, series, model, json_extract(properties,'$.wattage') AS wattage, json_extract(properties,'$.luminous_flux') AS luminous_flux FROM products WHERE brand='Osram' AND (name LIKE '%Warm Classic 2NL-Z7I%' OR series LIKE '%Warm Classic 2NL-Z7I%' OR model='2NL-Z7I') AND (name LIKE '%LED Bulb%' OR series LIKE '%LED Bulb%') AND json_extract(properties,'$.wattage') IN ('6 W','6W','6') AND json_extract(properties,'$.luminous_flux') IN ('1055 lm','1055lm','1055') LIMIT 10;
SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.lens_color') AS lens_color, json_extract(properties,'$.size') AS size, properties FROM products WHERE brand='3M' AND (series LIKE '%SecureFit%' OR name LIKE '%SecureFit%' OR model LIKE '%2CE-B35%') LIMIT 20;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2025-06-22
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Nut Bolt and Washer
Requested kind_id: nuts_bolts_washers

For today's report, use the normal catalogue count: count every product record
whose kind_id matches the requested product kind. Count each SKU once. Do not
apply inventory, display, merchandising, or family-hold filters unless another
current update explicitly names them.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

)"

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, path, name FROM stores
  WHERE city='Graz' AND name LIKE '%Jakomini%'
), candidates AS (
  SELECT 'p1_floor_cleaner' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='cleaning_liquids'
    AND p.kind_id='cleaning_liquid'
    AND p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p2_degreaser' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='cleaning_liquids'
    AND p.kind_id='cleaning_liquid'
    AND p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p3_threaded_rod' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='nut_bolt_washers'
    AND p.kind_id='nut_bolt_and_washer'
    AND p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='threaded rod'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p4_tool_bag' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='tool_boxes_and_bags'
    AND p.kind_id='tool_box_and_bag'
    AND p.brand='Festool'
    AND p.series='Festool Stackable'
    AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p5_bolt_8mm' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='nut_bolt_washers'
    AND p.kind_id='nut_bolt_and_washer'
    AND p.brand='Heco'

    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'p6_parts_case' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='tool_boxes_and_bags'
    AND p.kind_id='tool_box_and_bag'
    AND p.brand='Festool'
    AND p.series='Festool Stackable'
    AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='parts case'
)
SELECT c.item_key, c.sku, c.path, ts.id AS store_id, i.available_today, ts.path AS store_path
FROM candidates c
CROSS JOIN target_store ts
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=ts.id AND i.sku=c.sku
ORDER BY c.item_key;

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
basket_lines,"CREATE TABLE basket_lines (
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (basket_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
baskets,"CREATE TABLE baskets (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	discount_percent INTEGER,
	discount_reason_code TEXT,
	discount_issuer_id TEXT,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
categories,"CREATE TABLE categories (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	department TEXT NOT NULL
)"
customers,"CREATE TABLE customers (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	home_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	home_lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
employee_roles,"CREATE TABLE employee_roles (
	employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	role TEXT NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (employee_id, role),
	FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employees(id)
)"
employees,"CREATE TABLE employees (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	title TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
families,"CREATE TABLE families (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payment_lines,"CREATE TABLE payment_lines (
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	unit_price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (payment_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payments,"CREATE TABLE payments (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_archived INTEGER NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_method_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	device_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	observed_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	observed_lon REAL NOT NULL,
	three_ds_status TEXT,
	three_ds_failure_reason TEXT,
	three_ds_attempts INTEGER,
	three_ds_max_attempts INTEGER,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
product_kinds,"CREATE TABLE product_kinds (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL
)"
product_properties,"CREATE TABLE product_properties (
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	key TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_text TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_number REAL,
	PRIMARY KEY (sku, key)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
returns,"CREATE TABLE returns (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	reason_code TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id)
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

WITH req AS (
  SELECT 'c1' cid, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Viega' AND series='Professional' AND model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN'
     AND name LIKE '%Pipe Fitting%'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.fitting_type')='compression coupler'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='12 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c2' cid, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Gardena' AND series='Smart' AND model='PowerMax 280-5FI'
     AND name LIKE '%Manual Garden Tool%'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='pressure sprayer'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c3' cid, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Bondex' AND series='Garden' AND model='Classic 106-TS1'
     AND name LIKE '%Wood Stain and Deck Oil%'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.product_type')='lacquer'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c4' cid, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Metabo' AND series='Brushless' AND model='BS 1OW-IK5'
     AND name LIKE '%Cordless Drill Driver%'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.voltage')='12 V'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.battery_platform')='12v-system'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.kit_contents')='case'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c5' cid, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Bahco' AND series='Workshop' AND model='BAH 3VZ-SPH'
     AND name LIKE '%Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool%'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='measuring tape'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c6' cid, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Viega' AND series='Professional' AND model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN'
     AND name LIKE '%Pipe Fitting%'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.fitting_type')='pipe fitting'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='15 mm'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.connection_type')='push fit'
)
SELECT r.cid, r.sku, r.path, i.available_today
FROM req r
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku=r.sku AND i.store_id='store_innsbruck_wilten'
ORDER BY r.cid;

  SELECT id, path, name FROM stores WHERE city='Graz' AND name LIKE '%Jakomini%'
)

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, path, name FROM stores
  WHERE city='Vienna' AND name LIKE '%Praterstern%'
  LIMIT 1
),
q1 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
  JOIN product_properties pp1 ON pp1.sku=p.sku AND pp1.key='color_family' AND pp1.value_text='White'
  JOIN product_properties pp2 ON pp2.sku=p.sku AND pp2.key='finish' AND pp2.value_text='satin'
  WHERE p.category_id='wall_paint' AND p.kind_id='wall_paint'
    AND p.brand='Helios' AND p.series='Helios Quick Dry Mobihel' AND p.model='1VV-8Y1'
),
q2 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
  JOIN product_properties pp1 ON pp1.sku=p.sku AND pp1.key='storage_type' AND pp1.value_text='storage bin'
  JOIN product_properties pp2 ON pp2.sku=p.sku AND pp2.key='color_family' AND pp2.value_text='Gray'
  JOIN product_properties pp3 ON pp3.sku=p.sku AND pp3.key='volume' AND pp3.value_text='8 l'
  WHERE p.category_id='storage_bin_and_organizer' AND p.kind_id='storage_bin_and_organizer'
    AND p.brand='Keter' AND p.series='Keter Compact Stack' AND p.model='99Z-RIS'
),
q3 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
  JOIN product_properties pp1 ON pp1.sku=p.sku AND pp1.key='length' AND pp1.value_text='550 mm'
  WHERE p.category_id='wiper_blade' AND p.kind_id='wiper_blade'
    AND p.brand='Michelin' AND p.series='Michelin Compact Radius' AND p.model='XBS-24G'
),
q4 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
  JOIN product_properties pp1 ON pp1.sku=p.sku AND pp1.key='protection_type' AND pp1.value_text='ear defenders'
  JOIN product_properties pp2 ON pp2.sku=p.sku AND pp2.key='color_family' AND pp2.value_text='Gray'
  WHERE p.category_id='head_and_hearing_protection' AND p.kind_id='head_and_hearing_protection'
    AND p.brand='3M' AND p.series='3M Comfort Aura' AND p.model='K2Y-9TP'
),
q5 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
  JOIN product_properties pp1 ON pp1.sku=p.sku AND pp1.key='color_family' AND pp1.value_text='Gray'
  JOIN product_properties pp2 ON pp2.sku=p.sku AND pp2.key='finish' AND pp2.value_text='satin'
  JOIN product_properties pp3 ON pp3.sku=p.sku AND pp3.key='volume' AND pp3.value_text='2500 ml'
  WHERE p.category_id='wall_paint' AND p.kind_id='wall_paint'
    AND p.brand='Helios' AND p.series='Helios Quick Dry Mobihel' AND p.model='1VV-8Y1'
),
q6 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
  JOIN product_properties pp1 ON pp1.sku=p.sku AND pp1.key='lens_color' AND pp1.value_text='Gray'
  JOIN product_properties pp2 ON pp2.sku=p.sku AND pp2.key='size' AND pp2.value_text='one size'
  JOIN product_properties pp3 ON pp3.sku=p.sku AND pp3.key='coating' AND pp3.value_text='anti scratch'
  WHERE p.category_id='safety_eyewear' AND p.kind_id='safety_eyewear'
    AND p.brand='Uvex' AND p.series='Uvex Industrial x-fit' AND p.model='3MR-QL3'
),
allq AS (
  SELECT 'q1' q, * FROM q1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q2' q, * FROM q2 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q3' q, * FROM q3 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q4' q, * FROM q4 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q5' q, * FROM q5 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q6' q, * FROM q6
)
SELECT q, sku, path, available_today FROM allq;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id FROM stores WHERE id='store_vienna_praterstern'
),
candidates AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE 
    (
      p.brand='Helios' AND p.series='Quick Dry' AND p.model='Mobihel 1VV-8Y1'
      AND p.name LIKE '%Wall Paint%'
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='color_family' AND x.value_text='White')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='finish' AND x.value_text='satin')
    )
    OR (
      p.brand='Keter' AND p.name LIKE '%Storage Bin and Organizer%'
      AND p.name LIKE '%99Z-RIS%'
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='storage_type' AND x.value_text='storage bin')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='color_family' AND x.value_text='Gray')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='volume' AND x.value_text='8 l')
    )
    OR (
      p.brand='Michelin' AND p.name LIKE '%Wiper Blade%'
      AND p.name LIKE '%XBS-24G%'
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='length' AND x.value_text='550 mm')
    )
    OR (
      p.brand='3M' AND p.series='Comfort' AND p.model='Aura K2Y-9TP'
      AND p.name LIKE '%Head and Hearing Protection%'
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='protection_type' AND x.value_text='ear defenders')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='color_family' AND x.value_text='Gray')
    )
    OR (
      p.brand='Helios' AND p.series='Quick Dry' AND p.model='Mobihel 1VV-8Y1'
      AND p.name LIKE '%Wall Paint%'
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='color_family' AND x.value_text='Gray')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='finish' AND x.value_text='satin')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='volume' AND x.value_text='2500 ml')
    )
    OR (
      p.brand='Uvex' AND p.series='Industrial' AND p.model='x-fit 3MR-QL3'
      AND p.name LIKE '%Safety Eyewear%'
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='lens_color' AND x.value_text='Gray')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='size' AND x.value_text='one size')
      AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties x WHERE x.sku=p.sku AND x.key='coating' AND x.value_text='anti scratch')
    )
)
SELECT c.sku, c.path, c.name, i.available_today
FROM candidates c
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=c.sku
JOIN target_store ts ON ts.id=i.store_id
ORDER BY c.sku;
SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('stores','products','inventory') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

SELECT 'store' AS rec_type, id, path, name, NULL AS sku FROM stores WHERE name='PowerTool Innsbruck Wilten'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'product', NULL, p.path, p.name, p.sku FROM products p WHERE p.brand='Wurth' AND p.series='Wurth Universal' AND p.model='ZEBRA 2RS-7GT' AND p.category_id='wood_drywall_screws' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=3 AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm')=30 AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.drive_type')='pozidriv'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'product', NULL, p.path, p.name, p.sku FROM products p WHERE p.brand='Delta Plus' AND p.series='Delta Plus Comfort' AND p.model='DP 26T-AN2' AND p.category_id='respiratory_protection' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.mask_type')='dust mask' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_class')='ffp2'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'product', NULL, p.path, p.name, p.sku FROM products p WHERE p.brand='Keter' AND p.series='Keter Connect' AND p.model='Stack 3NE-IBR' AND p.category_id='storage_bins_organizers' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='storage bin' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Clear'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'product', NULL, p.path, p.name, p.sku FROM products p WHERE p.brand='Mellerud' AND p.series='Mellerud Eco' AND p.model='MEL 1AA-TXA' AND p.category_id='cleaning_liquids' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='multi surface cleaner' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume_ml')=2500
UNION ALL
SELECT 'product', NULL, p.path, p.name, p.sku FROM products p WHERE p.brand='JSP' AND p.series='JSP Secure' AND p.model='EVO 1FW-2OJ' AND p.category_id='head_hearing_protection' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_type')='ear defenders' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Blue'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'product', NULL, p.path, p.name, p.sku FROM products p WHERE p.brand='Liqui Moly' AND p.series='Liqui Moly Professional' AND p.model='Special 3CD-T5N' AND p.category_id='engine_oil' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume_ml')=5000 AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.viscosity')='0W-20';

SELECT s.store_id, s.name, s.city, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today FROM stores s LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id = s.store_id AND i.sku = 'FST-23VT61XO' WHERE s.city = 'Vienna' ORDER BY s.store_id;

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('stores','inventory','products');

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
